source.title: Hippocampal neurons construct a map of an abstract value space

confidence_rationale: High-quality single-unit recording in primates (Cell, 2021). Shows directly that hippocampal neurons tile an abstract value space with place-like fields -- value encoding is not merely tagging but genuine map-structured representation. This is the strongest existing evidence that hippocampal cognitive maps extend to value geometry, directly supporting MECH-073 and challenging the ARC-007 framing that the hippocampal module is value-computation-free.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Primate hippocampal neurons exhibit 'value place fields' -- spatially tuned responses to positions in an abstract reward-value space defined by the relative value of three choice options. These fields share four properties with spatial place cells: consistency across experiences, multidimensional tuning, directional selectivity, and remapping in novel contexts. They generalize across contexts as experience accumulates.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-073 proposes valence is intrinsic to hippocampal map geometry; Knudsen and Wallis provide the most direct demonstration that the hippocampus actively *constructs* a value map, not merely receives one. The abstract value space maps directly onto REE's residue field R(x,t): R is not just geometrically tagged by external valence input but is the hippocampus's own representational structure. For ARC-007, the critical implication is that 'no new value computation' must be interpreted carefully -- the hippocampus does generate value-structured representations, but these may be retrieved rather than computed de novo on each planning step.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The value space here is purely abstract (relative option value in a choice task), not harm-avoidance or spatial-emotional geography. REE's residue field conflates harm, goal, and spatial salience. Whether value place fields would emerge for harm-related residue (vs. reward-relative-value) is untested. Primate choice-value task differs substantially from agent harm-navigation scenarios; transfer requires assuming the same mechanism operates across value types.
mapping.source_context: Primate (macaque) hippocampus; single-unit electrophysiology during abstract value-based decision task; cue values defined by relative reward magnitudes

confidence_components.notes: Cell paper with clean primate electrophysiology. Mapping fidelity reduced because value in the paper is purely abstract reward comparison, whereas MECH-073 and ARC-007 concern harm/goal valence in navigation context. The four-property parallelism with spatial place cells is strong but the value domain differs from REE's residue field.
